title: Add tests for unresolved startup and workspace_initialize(cwd) binding semantics
summary: Add focused tests covering unresolved startup when process CWD is '/', one-time workspace initialization binding, same-root reinitialization as a no-op, and conflicting rebind rejection for the v0.4.6 startup contract.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover server startup behavior when process CWD is '/' and the repo context begins unresolved instead of failing immediately.
Tests verify workspace_initialize(cwd) successfully binds a validated non-root project directory.
Tests verify calling workspace_initialize(cwd) again with the same root succeeds as a no-op.
Tests verify calling workspace_initialize(cwd) with '/' or with a different root after binding fails with clear errors.
Coverage contribution helps keep total project coverage at or above 90%.

steps.description: Identify the new startup and binding branches introduced by unresolved RepoContext initialization and workspace_initialize(cwd).
Add RepoContext tests covering unresolved startup state, initial bind, same-root rebind no-op, and different-root rebind rejection.
Add entrypoint/router or JSON-RPC tests confirming workspace_initialize(cwd) is exposed and enforces the expected binding semantics.
Run targeted tests to confirm the initialization-binding contract is protected by deterministic regression coverage.

notes: Keep tests hermetic and filesystem-local; do not rely on a real Zed process.
Prefer direct assertions on initialization state transitions and error messages over broad integration scaffolding.
This ticket focuses on initialization binding semantics; failed-tool logging is covered by p3-t3.
